issueContext.title: Strike-3 instrumentation skill should enforce hypothesis-before-fix discipline
issueContext.body: After AGENTS.md L1's strike-3 instrumentation deploys, the follow-up data-driven fix subagent currently jumps straight to writing a fix. It often misreads the data and produces fixes that pass local tests but miss the actual root cause. Evidence from cookbook UX-12 timer-overlay on 2026-06-04: Strike-3 instrumentation captured activeCount=1->0 at +33s in browser console; the fix subagent said code-read confirms no useEffect cleanup calls stopTimer, added regression tests, but missed the actual cause. Required another strike S-3b to find the bug, local useState in publisher. Proposed change: update the strike-3 instrumentation skill or canonical strike-3 prompt template to require 1. enumerate hypotheses before writing a fix, with 3+ candidate causes and a falsifying log line for each; 2. cite specific log lines by seq number when proposing a fix, with fallback when seq is unavailable; 3. reject any fix PR that does not cite at least one specific log line by marking needs-more-data.
commentsSummary: Triage confirmed this is a focused process-hardening feature, not covered by #798/#851. #798 enforced instrumentation-only Strike-3 iterations, but not post-log interpretation discipline.

Likely affected surfaces include diagnostic-first-phase.js/.md, docs/agent-reference/process-authoring.md, root-cause diagnosis/GSD debugging guidance, and possibly shared n-strikes escalation handoffs.
Searches did not find an existing enforceable contract for 3+ competing hypotheses, falsifying observations, concrete log citations, or needs-more-data rejection.
Risk is medium because prompt-only enforcement may be bypassed; mitigation is a concrete output schema/checklist plus regression guard.

acceptanceCriteria: Post-instrumentation fix agents must enumerate at least 3 candidate root-cause hypotheses before writing a fix.
Each candidate hypothesis must include a concrete falsifying observation or log line.
Any proposed fix must cite at least one specific log record: seq number when available, otherwise artifact path plus timestamp or exact log id.
A fix proposal without concrete log citation must be rejected or classified as needs-more-data.
The rule must be scoped to Strike-3/post-instrumentation or diagnostic-first handoffs, not every first-pass bugfix.
A regression guard must make the contract difficult to remove silently.
reuseAuditFindings: No database migrations, API routes, or environment variables are needed.
No SDK dependency is needed; existing @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk defineTask patterns are sufficient.
The canonical current implementation surface is diagnostic-first-phase.js/.md; no instrumentation-deploy skill file exists in the repository scan.
library/processes/shared/n-strikes-escalation.js is adjacent but should only be changed if the implementation maps a real Strike-3 handoff through it.
library/__tests__/orphan-preflight-prompts.test.mjs is the closest existing prompt-contract regression-test pattern.
